---
description: "WCAG 2.2 Level AA accessibility guidance — semantics, keyboard, forms, contrast, reflow"
applyTo: "**/*.svelte, **/*.ts, **/*.css, **/*.html"
---

# Accessibility Instructions

## Non-negotiables (MUST)

- Conform to WCAG 2.2 Level AA
- Prefer native HTML elements/attributes over ARIA
- Use ARIA only when necessary — don't add ARIA when native semantics already work
- Ensure correct accessible name, role, value, states, and properties
- All interactive elements are keyboard operable with visible focus and no traps
- Use respectful, inclusive, people-first language in user-facing text

## Page Structure

- Use landmarks (`header`, `nav`, `main`, `footer`) appropriately
- Use headings to introduce sections; avoid skipping heading levels
- Prefer one `h1` for the page topic
- Set a descriptive `<title>` — prefer "Unique page - section - site"

## Keyboard and Focus

- All interactive elements keyboard operable
- Tab order follows reading order predictably
- Focus is always visible
- Hidden content is not focusable
- Provide a skip link as the first focusable element

## Controls and Labels

- Every interactive element has a visible label
- Labels don't disappear while entering text
- Accessible name contains the visible label text
- Multiple controls with same label get `aria-label` with added context

## Forms

- Every form control has a programmatic label (prefer `<label for="...">`)
- Indicate required fields visually and with `aria-required="true"`
- Use `aria-invalid="true"` for invalid fields; associate errors with `aria-describedby`
- On submit with invalid input, focus the first invalid control

## Contrast and Visual

- Text contrast: at least 4.5:1 (large text: 3:1)
- Focus indicators and control boundaries: at least 3:1
- Don't rely on color alone — provide text and/or icons
- Use project design tokens for colors — avoid arbitrary hex values

## Reflow (320px)

- Multi-line text fits within 320px containers without horizontal scrolling
- Multi-column layouts stack into single column at narrow widths
- All interactive elements remain visible and operable

## Images and Graphics

- Informative graphics have meaningful alternatives (`alt`, `aria-label`)
- Decorative graphics are hidden (`alt=""`, `aria-hidden="true"`)

## Verification Checklist

Before finalizing, verify:
- [ ] Landmarks, headings, and one `h1`
- [ ] Keyboard operable, visible focus, no traps, skip link
- [ ] Visible labels in accessible names
- [ ] Forms: labels, required, errors with aria-invalid
- [ ] Contrast meets thresholds
- [ ] Reflow at 320px without content loss
- [ ] Informative alt text, decorative hidden
